{"id":"obelisk-b45","title":"Token in/out parsing broken — ANSI escapes and chunk boundaries break regex","description":"Token parsing in on_agent_pty_data() (app.rs:2077-2087) has multiple issues:

1. ANSI escape sequences break regex: The regex runs against raw PTY bytes which contain ANSI color/cursor codes. Claude Code renders token counts with escape sequences interspersed (e.g. ESC[38;5;240mInput tokensESC[0m: ESC[1m12,345), so the regex pattern 'input[_ ]tokens?[:\s]+([0-9][0-9,]*)' fails to match because [:\s]+ can't skip over escape codes between the label and number.

2. No ANSI stripping: There is no strip_ansi function anywhere in the codebase. Raw PTY bytes go directly to regex matching.

3. Chunk boundary splitting: Parsing runs per ~4KB PTY data chunk. If a token count line is split across two chunks, it's missed entirely.

4. Format mismatch: Claude Code's actual token output format may not match the assumed 'input tokens: N' pattern.

Fix: Strip ANSI escapes before regex matching (e.g. use the strip-ansi-escapes crate), and either buffer text across chunks or parse from the vt100 screen contents instead of raw bytes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:08:14Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:08:14Z"}

{"id":"obelisk-deh","title":"Poll loop hardcodes 30s sleep, ignores poll_interval_secs config","description":"The background poll loop in main.rs:119 hardcodes 'tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(30))' instead of using the configured poll_interval_secs from obelisk.toml. The UI countdown timer correctly uses the config value (app.rs:473, 726, 750), so when poll_interval_secs is set to anything other than 30 (e.g. 15), the countdown and actual poll cycle desync — the gauge counts down to 0 and resets but no poll result arrives until the hardcoded 30s elapses. Fix: pass poll_interval_secs into the poller task and use it for the sleep duration.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:24:23Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:24:23Z"}

new_value: {"description":"Despite the obelisk-frt fix (which added dynamic ESC[6n interception in on_agent_pty_data), ^[[1;1R still appears as visible text at agent startup on macOS. The likely cause: Claude Code on macOS sends ESC[6n during its own startup, triggering the dynamic CPR response in app.rs:2101-2104. The CPR (ESC[1;1R) is written to the PTY stdin, but Claude Code doesn't consume it silently — it echoes back as literal text in the terminal output. The fix needs to also strip/filter the CPR response from the PTY output stream before feeding it to the vt100 parser, or avoid sending the CPR entirely on non-Windows platforms even when ESC[6n is detected.","title":"CPR response ^[[1;1R still leaks on macOS despite obelisk-frt fix"}
old_value: {"id":"obelisk-zu0","title":"Garbled rendering of Claude Code startup banner graphics","description":"Claude Code's startup banner includes graphic characters (logo/mascot) that render as garbled blocks in the obelisk terminal view. The vt100 parser (vt100 0.16) likely doesn't support the image/graphics protocol being used (possibly Kitty graphics protocol, iTerm2 inline images, or Unicode private-use-area characters). These appear as corrupted orange/brown blocks in the PTY render area. Additionally, ^[[1;1R still appears as visible text despite obelisk-frt fix — either Claude Code on macOS sends ESC[6n at startup (triggering the dynamic CPR response which then leaks back as visible text), or the fix needs to strip the CPR from the PTY output before feeding it to the vt100 parser.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:28:54Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:28:54Z"}

old_value: {"id":"obelisk-rc1","title":"Completion bar calculation is wrong: numerator/denominator mismatch","description":"The completion_rate() function uses total_completed (monotonic lifetime counter) as numerator but agents.len() (dynamic current list size) as denominator. This causes multiple bugs: (1) Dismissing finished agents inflates the percentage (e.g. 10 agents, 5 complete = 50%, dismiss the 5 completed ones → 5/5 = 100%). (2) Retried/failed agents deflate the percentage by inflating the denominator. (3) Failed agents are never counted in the numerator but always in the denominator. Fix: use a consistent basis — either both counters are cumulative (total_completed / total_spawned) or both are snapshot-based (count agents with Completed status / agents.len()).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T05:34:55Z","created_by":"Chanjun Park","updated_at":"2026-03-13T05:34:55Z"}

{"id":"obelisk-dh3","title":"Sequential merge queue to prevent concurrent merge conflicts","description":"When multiple agents finish near-simultaneously and attempt to merge to main, the second merge will fail due to conflicts with the first. Implement a merge queue that serializes merge operations: when an agent reaches the merging phase, it acquires a lock (file-based or in-memory), performs the merge, and releases. Other agents wait and rebase before their turn. Should handle the case where a queued agent's merge genuinely conflicts (not just a race) by surfacing the conflict in the event log rather than silently failing.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-14T00:03:56Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-14T00:03:56Z"}

old_value: {"id":"obelisk-ip4","title":"Detect agent completion by polling beads issue status instead of PTY scraping","description":"Currently obelisk detects the Done phase by string-matching PTY output for '\"status\": \"closed\"' after seeing 'bd close'. This is fragile — it depends on exact JSON formatting in terminal output and can miss completions if the output scrolls differently or the agent closes the issue through an unexpected code path. Instead, obelisk should periodically poll 'bd show \u003cissue-id\u003e --json' for each running agent and transition to Done when the issue status is 'closed'. This decouples completion detection from terminal output parsing entirely. The existing phase detection for earlier phases (Claiming, Worktree, Implementing, etc.) can remain as PTY heuristics since they're informational, but the actual completion signal should come from the source of truth: beads.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-14T00:12:43Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-14T00:12:43Z"}
